title: Podagrostis thurberiana
taxonomy: Podagrostis thurberiana is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is classified in the subfamily Pooideae and tribe Hordeae. This species is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Podagrostis thurberiana is further divided into the sections Thurberianae and Pooidaeae. It is closely related to other species within the genus Podagrostis.

intro: Podagrostis thurberiana, also known as Thurber's grass or Thurber's needle grass, is a perennial grass species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Podagrostis thurberiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used to make smoking mixtures and cough syrups.
